At its next meeting the COM
mission that is considering the re-
vision of the city charter will again
take up the question of electing
councilmen at large instead of by
wards as at present, and 1t is be-
lieved will approve the proposition.
Since its consideration at former
meeting, when the amendment lack
ed one vote of receiving the approx
al of the required majority or II
members of the commission, 1t has
been changed to some extent In Or
der to meet the objections brought
against 1t as originally drafted. The
requirement Is made that the can
didate for councilman shall have
lived in the city at least two years
and shall have been a property
owner at least one year.


The expiration of the terms OF
councilmen is made to take place as
under the present charter. five each
year. thereby insuring one-half of
the council being familiar with the
work. The first election under the
new provision. IL finally approved.
will occur In 1911. therefore 1t will
not affect the councilmen to be
elected next May. The amendment
as revised reads;
